name: A Tour of the Badlands by mountain bike
name_original: Una vuelta por Badlands en BTT
description:
The Cárcavas de Marchal, with their impressive earth formations sculpted by erosion, seem like a landscape from another world, where the ravines and earthy colors create a unique and overwhelming natural spectacle.

Purullena is the starting point for this circular route. You can easily get there by car from the A92 motorway and use the authorized parking spaces available in the municipality. During the Route you pass through several towns where it is possible to find supplies, although it does not hurt to bring a snack to eat during the itinerary. Along the route, you will come across natural viewpoints of the gullies where it will be difficult to avoid stopping to enjoy the spectacle.

The Route runs, for the most part, along roads and paths. In some sections there are reguerones that add a certain degree of fun to the adventure, always with caution. Summer is very hot, although if it is your favorite season you can always pedal first thing in the morning to avoid the stifling heat. Additionally, rain can add a degree of difficulty to the route, since you are on clay terrain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Purullena offers routes through a distinctive landscape characterized by badlands formations, arid riverbeds, and dramatic canyons. The region's terrain features unique geological structures, including the "cárcavas" or gullies, and views over the Hoya de Guadix. Mountain bike trails often follow ancient paths and offer a mix of open, exposed sections and routes near reservoirs. Best mountain bike trails around Purullena The most popular mountain bike trail is A Tour of the Badlands by mountain bike, a 27.6 miles (44.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete, traversing the unique badlands scenery. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cárcavas de Marchal – circular desde Benalúa por la Hoya de Guadix, a moderate 32.9 miles (53.0 km) path. This route explores the distinctive gullies and offers expansive views of the Hoya de Guadix. Local mountain bikers also love the Belerda Canyon and Francisco Abellán Reservoir Loop, a 30.2 miles (48.6 km) trail leading through canyons and alongside a reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around Purullena is defined by badlands formations, dry riverbeds, and scenic canyons. The network includes 3 easy, 52 moderate, and 9 difficult routes, offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Purullena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Purullena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Purullena?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Purullena, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The network includes 3 easy, 52 moderate, and 9 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Purullena?
A: Yes, Purullena offers a few easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and shorter distances, allowing new riders to enjoy the unique badlands scenery comfortably.

Q: What are the options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: For advanced mountain bikers, there are 9 difficult routes around Purullena. These trails often feature more significant elevation changes, technical sections, and longer distances, providing a demanding ride through the region's dramatic canyons and arid landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Purullena?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, the 3 easy trails available would be the most suitable starting point for families. These routes offer a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the area's distinctive terrain.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Purullena?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes in Purullena are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1150675261/carcavas-de-marchal-circular-desde-benalua-por-la-hoya-de-guadix" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cárcavas de Marchal – circular desde Benalúa por la Hoya de Guadix&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the distinctive gullies and offers expansive views.

Q: What kind of scenic viewpoints can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Purullena offer numerous scenic viewpoints overlooking the unique badlands formations, the Hoya de Guadix, and dramatic canyons. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Badlands de Purullena Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3886587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cárcavas de Marchal&lt;/a&gt;. You can experience these on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31538574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hoya de Guadix – Badlands de Purullena Viewpoint loop from Purullena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Purullena?
A: The best time for mountain biking in Purullena is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot due to the arid climate, while winters can bring cooler temperatures, though snow is rare in the lower badlands areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Purullena?
A: The mountain bike trails in Purullena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the unique badlands scenery, the dramatic canyons, and the sense of adventure offered by the arid riverbeds and geological formations.

Q: Are there any interesting natural attractions or landmarks along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the routes often pass by or offer views of significant natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granada Geopark&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6359134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;End of the World Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Hoya de Guadix&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also lead through impressive gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6399081" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Purullena Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails near Purullena typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails near Purullena varies depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate trails, which are the most common, can range from around 2 hours 50 minutes for a 32 km route to over 4 hours for longer, 50+ km routes. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494769555/a-tour-of-the-badlands-by-mountain-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Tour of the Badlands by mountain bike&lt;/a&gt; takes approximately 3 hours 35 minutes.

Q: Is public transport an option for accessing mountain bike trails in Purullena?
A: Public transport options directly to trailheads in the more remote badlands areas may be limited. It is generally recommended to use a car for convenient access to the starting points of most mountain bike routes around Purullena, especially for carrying your bike.

Q: Are there less crowded mountain bike trails for a more solitary experience?
A: While some popular routes can see more activity, the extensive network of 64 trails means it's possible to find less crowded options, especially on weekdays or by exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate and difficult routes. The vastness of the badlands also offers a sense of solitude on many paths.
tips: This viewpoint has become an outstanding place to watch the sunrise and sunset.

This clay massif of reddish and yellowish hues is formed from the movements of the Earth's plates, which caused the area to sink 25 million years ago. Erosion due to its particular climate leaves a unique landscape, characterized by ravines, rills and deep ditches. Without a doubt, it is worthy of admiration.
La Hoya de Guadix is a surprising plain where you can enjoy the path and its views of the ravines and a unique landscape marked by the erosion of the Fardes and Guadix river basins.

The La Peza viewpoint on the GR-4104 is a wonderful place to admire the panoramic views of the village and the surrounding Sierra Nevada. It is situated at an altitude of around 1000 metres and offers magnificent views over La Peza and the valley below.
Along this track, if you look up, you can see the peaks of the Sierra Nevada rising majestically above the hills and foothills.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of the Badlands of Purullena
